content: Just like Leonard from *Memento* relies on external notes and tattoos to compensate for anterograde amnesia and pursue his long-term goal, current computer automation agents rely on persistent external memory and explicitly encoded action prompts to compensate for their own lack of native long-term contextual memory when executing multi-step long-horizon tasks. Building automation agents around Nolan's 'memento framework' of fragmented external memory anchoring can improve their ability to complete extended open-ended tasks without coherent native memory retention.
